note: IB Diploma Computer Science HL (established syllabus). Core topics 1-4 (system fundamentals, computer organization, networks, computational thinking & programming), HL extension topics 5-7 (abstract data structures, resource management, control), plus the Object-Oriented Programming option. Lessons are curated concept clusters; codes 1.1-8.3 sort in syllabus order. Factual syllabus structure only.

topics.name: Systems in organizations: planning and change management
System design basics: components, usability and documentation
Computer architecture: the CPU and primary memory
Secondary memory and operating systems
Binary representation of data
Network fundamentals
Data transmission and wireless networks
Computational thinking: abstraction, decomposition and algorithms
Connecting computational thinking and program design
Introduction to programming: variables, control and methods
Flowcharts, pseudocode and algorithm design
Abstract data structures: recursion and the stack
Linked lists
Trees and binary trees
Stacks, queues and the application of data structures
Resource management: system resources and scheduling
Memory management and virtual memory
Control systems: sensors, actuators and feedback
Embedded systems and microprocessors
Object-oriented programming: classes, objects and encapsulation
Inheritance, polymorphism and abstraction
OOP program design and UML
